reference: Hello there. Having a lump on a bending knee may be due to soft tissue, not bone, so it may not be visible on X-ray. It may be a torn muscle or a lax muscle, or it may be due to a herniated part of the joint. It would be better for you to first undergo a simple investigation like an ultrasound, and then if needed, a Magnetic Resonance Imaging scan may be the most confirmatory.
text: Hello there. Having a lump on a bending knee may be due to soft tissue, not bone, so it may not be visible on x-ray. It may be a torn muscle or a lax muscle, or it may be due to a herniated part of the joint. It would be better for you to first undergo a simple investigation like an ultrasound, and then, if needed, a magnetic resonance imaging scan may be the most confirmatory.
